violations.finding: Changed hunk introduces bare `python3 -` heredoc invocation. Line 35 (`python3 - <<'WGEOF'`) is in the diff-changed range (replaced an inline python3 -c block). No PYTHON_BIN resolver is present in this file; pretool-bash-safety.sh correctly uses `PYTHON_BIN="${CLAUDE_PYTHON_BIN:-${CLAUDE_HOME}/venv/bin/python}"` as the reference pattern.
violations.recommendation: Add a PYTHON_BIN resolver block at the top of pretool-write-guard.sh mirroring pretool-bash-safety.sh lines 10-13: `PYTHON_BIN="${CLAUDE_PYTHON_BIN:-${CLAUDE_HOME}/venv/bin/python}"; if [ ! -x "$PYTHON_BIN" ]; then PYTHON_BIN="${CLAUDE_PYTHON_FALLBACK:-python}"; fi`. Replace the line 35 invocation with `"$PYTHON_BIN" - <<'WGEOF'`.
violations.diff_overlap: confirmed_new — line 35 is within the changed hunk of this cycle's diff (e78ce03 vs 9ee76d8)

advisory_pre_existing.finding: Pre-existing bare `python3` calls on lines 10, 18, 22, and 50 — not touched by this diff. These lines are outside the changed hunk and pre-date this cycle. Same remediation as the line 35 finding applies (add PYTHON_BIN resolver, replace all python3 calls).
advisory_pre_existing.note: Not a current-cycle blocker per AC-2.6(b). Should be addressed in a dedicated cleanup cycle.

advisory_pre_existing.finding: Module-level docstring contains Chinese text: prompt `全部commit push` (verbatim user prompt from session 962de59f-fe0b-416e-b88b-7345fdf569e2). This is a pre-existing baseline line; the current cycle's changes to this file are in different hunks (function-level refactoring, not docstring).
advisory_pre_existing.recommendation: Move the verbatim Chinese quote to a docs/dev/ticket-<task-id>.md artifact. Replace the inline text with an English paraphrase plus ticket reference: `prompt: 'commit and push all' (verbatim: see docs/dev/ticket-b5d447e-incident.md)`.
advisory_pre_existing.note: Pre-existing; not introduced by this diff. Non-blocking under AC-2.6(b). Previously documented in style-inspector-report-20260518-094616.json and style-inspector-report-20260517-222440.json.

advisory_pre_existing.finding: User-visible BLOCKED error message contains Chinese: `BLOCKED: 必须使用正常的UI流程创建session，永远不允许使用代码创建session！`. Pre-existing baseline; current cycle's changes to this file did not touch this line (line number may have shifted due to earlier-hunk removals).
advisory_pre_existing.recommendation: Replace with English: `BLOCKED: Sessions must be created through the normal UI flow; creating sessions via code/API is never allowed.` If preserving the original user directive verbatim, create a docs/dev/ticket-<task-id>.md artifact and replace inline text with English paraphrase plus task-id attribution.
advisory_pre_existing.note: Pre-existing; previously documented in style-inspector-report-20260518-071337.json. Non-blocking under AC-2.6(b).

advisory_pre_existing.finding: Unconditional Standard 6 scan of agents/commands directories detected Chinese text at three locations. Line 253 contains Chinese in a USER_VERBATIM tag (`又`, `还是`, `没修好`, `第 N 次`). Lines 1400 and 1402 are also inside a USER_VERBATIM block (lines 1399-1403). Note: USER_VERBATIM is not a Standard 6 exemption for command files; verbatim non-English user-binding quotes must reside in docs/dev/ticket-*.md only, not in command/agent prompt files. All three locations are pre-existing and outside the changed hook-file set.
advisory_pre_existing.recommendation: Move verbatim Chinese user quotes to docs/dev/ticket-*.md artifacts. Replace the command-file text with English paraphrases plus ticket references. This has been identified and documented in multiple prior cycles (style-inspector-report-20260517-214922.json, style-inspector-report-20260517-172600.json, style-inspector-report-20260517-215114.json).
advisory_pre_existing.note: Standard 6 unconditional scan exception applies. These are pre-existing in commands/dev.md and not introduced by this cycle. Non-blocking under AC-2.6(b) — overlap-only proxy used (pre-diff baseline comparison not performed inline; close.md should apply line-level diff-overlap + baseline check).

standards_passed: no-inline-code (hooks/lib/allowlist.py, hooks/pretool-git-privilege-guard.py, hooks/posttool-allowlist-consume.py, hooks/tests/test_allowlist_consolidation.py — Python hook files, not command/agent .md files; standard N/A)

no-hardcoded-values (no hardcoded URLs, file paths, or environment-specific values beyond intentional protocol constants keyed by runtime SID in /tmp; all configurable values use env-var fallback pattern)
meaningful-naming (all filenames are descriptive; no enhance-*, fast-*, v2, etc. patterns)

english-only (changed lines in hooks/lib/allowlist.py, hooks/posttool-allowlist-consume.py, hooks/tests/test_allowlist_consolidation.py — all English; unconditional scan of agents/commands dirs performed per Standard 6 exception)
merge-similar-scripts (N/A — changed files are hook scripts, not scripts/ directory; no similar script duplication detected)
concise-documentation (N/A — no .md command/agent files in changed set)
parameterized-scripts (hooks/lib/allowlist.py and hooks/posttool-allowlist-consume.py accept all variable values via function args/stdin; no hardcoded operational values)
dangling-references (all imports verified: lib/subagent.py exists, lib/allowlist.py exists; no missing script references)
merge-conflicts (no merge conflict markers found in any changed file)

codex_consult.feedback_summary: Codex identified 5 issues: (1) Standard 3 finding over-scoped — lines 10,18,22,50 are pre-existing; only line 35 (the changed hunk) is new; (2) pretool-git-privilege-guard.py:11 is a module-level docstring (not comment) and is pre-existing — reclassify to advisory_pre_existing; (3) pretool-bash-safety.sh:757 Chinese message is pre-existing — reclassify to advisory_pre_existing; (4) commands/dev.md finding was internally inconsistent — lines 1400/1402 ARE inside USER_VERBATIM, but USER_VERBATIM is not a Standard 6 exemption for command files; all are pre-existing; (5) severity/blocking classification should separate actionable from pre-existing. One OBSERVATION_ONLY: no missed critical violations in the changed hook files beyond what was already covered.

findings.issue: Standard 3 finding over-scoped: lines 10,18,22,50 are pre-existing unchanged lines; only line 35 is in the changed hunk
findings.proposed_fix: Rewrite as: Standard 3 — hooks/pretool-write-guard.sh:35 uses bare python3 in the changed /allow shim. Lines 10,18,22,50 are pre-existing baseline and should be advisory_pre_existing.
findings.rationale: Codex correctly identified the scope boundary. Line 35 is the changed hunk; lines 10/18/22/50 are pre-existing. Report restructured accordingly.

findings.issue: pretool-git-privilege-guard.py:11 is module-level docstring, not code comment; and is pre-existing baseline text not introduced by this diff
findings.proposed_fix: Move to advisory_pre_existing; update wording from 'code comment' to 'module-level docstring'
findings.rationale: Codex is correct on both counts. Reclassified and rewording applied.

findings.issue: pretool-bash-safety.sh:757 Chinese BLOCKED message is pre-existing; line number may have shifted but content was not introduced by this diff
findings.proposed_fix: Move to advisory_pre_existing with improved English replacement recommendation
findings.rationale: Codex is correct. Reclassified to advisory_pre_existing.

findings.issue: commands/dev.md finding was internally inconsistent: draft claimed 1400/1402 are 'outside USER_VERBATIM' but they are inside USER_VERBATIM block 1399-1403
findings.proposed_fix: Correct to: lines 1400/1402 are inside USER_VERBATIM, but USER_VERBATIM is not a Standard 6 exemption for command files. All are pre-existing advisory.
findings.rationale: Codex correctly identified the factual error in the draft. USER_VERBATIM wrapping does not exempt command files from Standard 6. Wording corrected and reclassified advisory_pre_existing.

findings.issue: Severity/blocking classification should separate actionable from pre-existing; pre-existing findings should not appear in violations[] as cycle blockers
findings.proposed_fix: Use violations[] only for new/changed-hunk findings; move pre-existing to advisory_pre_existing[]
findings.rationale: Report structure updated to separate violations (new/changed-hunk) from advisory_pre_existing (pre-existing baseline).

findings.issue: No missed critical violations in the listed changed hook files beyond the pretool-write-guard.sh:35 issue already covered by draft item 1
findings.rationale: Confirms completeness of the violations scan.

codex_consult.feedback_incorporated: Applied all 5 blocker findings: (1) scoped Standard 3 violation to changed-hunk only (line 35); moved lines 10/18/22/50 to advisory_pre_existing; (2) corrected 'code comment' to 'module-level docstring' for pretool-git-privilege-guard.py:11 and reclassified to advisory_pre_existing; (3) reclassified pretool-bash-safety.sh:757 to advisory_pre_existing with improved English replacement recommendation; (4) corrected factual error about dev.md:1400/1402 being 'outside USER_VERBATIM' (they are inside, but USER_VERBATIM is not an exemption); reclassified all three to advisory_pre_existing; (5) restructured report to use violations[] for cycle-new findings only and advisory_pre_existing[] for pre-existing findings.
